strategy: Bull Call Spread $19/$25 Aug 2026 — precious metals bull market + New Gold acquisition transforms earnings power; IV >60% mandates spread

wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX elevated ~20+; gold/silver miners carry significant commodity-linked IV — CDE options premiums are strong. Conditions support wheel entry at current price levels.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Lower Bollinger Band estimated ~$16.00–$16.50 post-Q1 surge. Stock closed $18.75 after an 8.2% post-earnings move — above the lower band. Enter CSP on any pullback toward $16.50.

csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. Hard stop before ~August 1 (5 days before est. Q2 2026 earnings in early August).
csp.note: The $16 CSP is 14.7% below current price — substantial buffer. New Gold acquisition and >$2B projected FCF for FY2026 provide strong fundamental support at these levels.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. Do not hold through earnings.
coveredCall.note: If assigned at $16 CSP, sell $20 CC to reduce cost basis to ~$14.80. Significant premium at elevated gold/silver IV.

expiryComparison.iv: ~60% (precious metals miner; elevated by gold/silver bull market and recent acquisition)

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter on any pullback below $18.50 or after stock consolidates above $18.00 on a closing basis. Post-Q1 earnings IV settling — this is the entry window.
exitRules.profitExit: Close Contract 1 when spread reaches 50% of max gain (~$210 on $19/$25). Let Contract 2 ride to $25 analyst target. Take partial profits as gold/silver price rallies create momentum.
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not moved above $20.50 by July 1 (50% of DTE), close position. Precious metals can consolidate for extended periods — don't overpay for decaying theta.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 40–50% loss on net debit paid (close at ~$0.90–$1.00 loss on $1.80 net paid). Metal price reversal could be sudden.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close ALL positions by August 1, 2026 — 5 days before estimated Q2 2026 earnings. Miner stocks move violently on production updates. No exceptions.

options.flatScenario: Stock stays at $18.75 — spread expires near worthless. Loss: ~$180 per spread.
options.atTarget: Stock at $25.00 by August 1 — spread worth ~$5.50–6.00. Gain: ~$370–420 per spread (~205–230% return).
options.bestFor: Optimal: 85 days working time before earnings, cheapest premium for the same close window.

options.flatScenario: Same earnings close deadline — paying $0.10 extra per spread for unusable post-earnings time.
options.atTarget: Identical outcome at August 1 close. Extra cost with no benefit over the Aug expiry.
options.bestFor: Only if Q2 earnings are pushed past August — provides date slippage protection.

options.flatScenario: Paying $0.40 extra vs Aug for time that is cut off by the hard stop. Waste.
options.atTarget: Same close outcome — Aug is strictly optimal.
options.bestFor: Not recommended. Thesis resolves at Q2 earnings — use Aug expiry.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — $19/$25 Aug 15, 2026
recommendation.type: Bull Call Spread (IV ~60% — at the upper threshold; spread saves $1.10 vs single call and mandatory at this IV per methodology)
recommendation.entryNote: IV at ~60% hits the spread threshold — the $25 short leg saves $1.10 per contract vs single call. Stock surged 8.2% post-Q1 on record earnings: revenue +138% YoY (New Gold acquisition added Rainy River and New Afton mines), net income +640% YoY, net cash position ($843M cash vs $761M debt). Management projects >$3B EBITDA and >$2B FCF for full-year 2026 at current metal prices. $750M share buyback shows board conviction. $4,383/oz gold and $82.85/oz silver provide powerful earnings leverage — each $100 move in gold adds ~$40M+ quarterly to earnings at current production.

companyOverview: Coeur Mining is a US-headquartered gold and silver producer with operations across North America. The company underwent a major transformation in March 2026, closing the acquisition of New Gold Inc. — adding the New Afton copper-gold mine (British Columbia) and Rainy River gold mine (Ontario) to its existing portfolio of Palmarejo (Mexico), Rochester (Nevada), Kensington (Alaska), and Wharf (South Dakota). The result: Q1 2026 revenue nearly tripled to $856.2M, adj. EBITDA hit $474.9M, and the company swung to a net cash position ($843M cash, $761M debt). With gold at $4,383/oz and silver at $82.85/oz, Coeur is generating historic cash flows. Management projects >$3B EBITDA and >$2B free cash flow for FY2026. The $750M share repurchase programme represents ~5% of market cap — a strong capital return signal.

financials.note: ~4.3x projected FY2026 EBITDA — cheap for a gold major
+137.8% YoY — New Gold acquisition nearly tripled scale
Record quarter; strong margin expansion post-acquisition
+403.9% YoY — cash generative at current metal prices
$843.2M cash vs $761M total debt — strongest balance sheet in years
At budget gold/silver prices; upside if metals hold current levels

insiderFlow.summary: Minimal insider activity — only one directional sale in six months, and at a lower price than current levels.

transactions.insider: Kenneth J. Watkinson (VP Corporate Controller & CAO)
transactions.note: Sold below current $18.75 price — likely pre-Q1 earnings, routine position management. Small sale (VP level, not C-suite). No offsetting sales from CEO, CFO, or board since the New Gold close.

insiderFlow.signal: BULLISH — single small VP-level sale at $15.05, well below current price. Zero insider sales from CEO, CFO, or board members. Expanded $750M buyback programme is the strongest possible insider-equivalent signal — the board is buying aggressively at these prices.

catalysts: New Gold acquisition (closed March 20, 2026): New Afton and Rainy River add two Tier-1 Canadian mines — revenue nearly tripled in Q1 alone
$750M share repurchase programme (~5.8% of market cap) at current prices — board conviction at its most direct
FY2026 projected >$3B EBITDA and >$2B FCF at current metal prices ($4,383 gold, $82.85 silver)
Gold and silver bull market: gold up ~60% in the past 12 months; silver a further leverage play
Largest exploration investment in company history planned for 2026: Silvertip silver project in BC could define next production growth leg
Net cash position achieved in Q1 — balance sheet transformation opens door to further M&A or accelerated buybacks
risks: EPS miss in Q1 ($0.35 vs $0.50 estimate) — likely purchase price allocation adjustments from New Gold deal, but management credibility needs Q2 follow-through
Metal price exposure: a reversal in gold ($4,383/oz) or silver ($82.85/oz) directly compresses all EBITDA/FCF projections
New Gold integration risk: two new mines (Rainy River, New Afton) added operational complexity; early quarters post-acquisition often carry surprises
Dilution from New Gold acquisition: share count increased significantly; diluted EPS will lag revenue growth
Exploration investment risk: 2026 is the 'largest exploration investment in company history' — capital is deployed ahead of discovery
dilution: Significant share issuance occurred to fund and close the New Gold acquisition (March 2026). Diluted share count increased materially. However, the $750M buyback programme directly offsets dilution — the company is buying back more shares than typical SBC-level grants. Net dilution impact for ongoing shareholders is being actively managed.

strikeTable.atTarget: Above analyst target — very low probability
